title: lepidium tiehmii
intro: Lepidium tiehmii, also known as Tiehm's peppergrass, is a species of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to the states of California and Nevada in the United States. The plant is rich in glucosinolates, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Lepidium tiehmii has been traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to aid digestion and relieve respiratory issues, offering potential health benefits to those who consume it.
